perturbation.original_text: SHPS shall provide the Care Management Services directly to the Clients in accordance with the terms of the Client Agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: SHPS shall provide the Care Management Services directly to the Clients, adhering to the general guidelines of the Client Agreements.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces 'in accordance with the terms of the Client Agreements' with 'adhering to the general guidelines of the Client Agreements,' weakening SHPS's obligation. While the original text mandates strict adherence, the altered text allows for interpretation and deviation. This contradicts the overall intent of the agreement, which implies strict adherence. This creates an in-text contradiction, because the original text mandates strict adherence, but the changed text allows for interpretation. Specifically contradicting Section 1.3 which refers to 'defaults in the performance of any of its duties or obligations hereunder'. If the service is not provided in accordance to the client agreement it could be argued if this is a default, when the guideline are adhered to but not the specific agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that either party materially or repeatedly defaults in the performance of any of its duties or obligations hereunder and does not substantially cure such default within thirty days after being given written notice specifying the default, or, with respect to those defaults which cannot reasonably be cured within thirty days, if the defaulting party fails to proceed promptly after being given such notice to commence curing the default and thereafter to reasonably proceed to cure the same, then the party not in default 2 may, by giving written notice to the defaulting party, terminate this Agreement as of a date specified in such notice of termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation of SHPS from strict adherence to client agreements to merely adhering to 'general guidelines'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because Section 1.3 allows for termination upon 'defaults in the performance of any of its duties or obligations hereunder'. If SHPS deviates from the specific terms of the Client Agreements while only adhering to the 'general guidelines,' it creates ambiguity as to whether such deviation constitutes a 'default' under the agreement, potentially hindering the enforceability of the termination clause and clearly establishing a contradiction between the obligation to act in accordance with the agreements and what constitutes default.

perturbation.original_text: HPS will pay to SHPS all Care Management Revenues collected from such New Client, and SHPS will pay a commission to HPS equal to five percent (5%) of such amount received by SHPS from HPS pursuant to this Section 2.4.
perturbation.changed_text: HPS will pay to SHPS the majority of Care Management Revenues collected from such New Client, and SHPS may pay a commission to HPS equivalent to approximately five percent (5%) of such amount received by SHPS from HPS pursuant to this Section 2.4.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'all' and 'equal' are replaced by 'majority' and 'approximately'. This introduces ambiguity into the revenue sharing and commission calculation for new clients. The exact percentage is vague and the amount to be paid is vague, impacting the predictability of payments. This contradicts the precise payment terms outlined elsewhere in Section 2. Section 2.1 states 'For each month during the term of this Agreement, HPS will pay to SHPS an amount equal to' highlighting a requirement for precision. This is an in-text contradiction because the amendment introduces inexact payment specifications while the initial agreement requires precise payment calculations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For each month during the term of this Agreement, HPS will pay to SHPS an amount equal to (i) eighty-two and one-half percent (82.5%) of the first $500,000 of Care Management Revenues (as defined below) during such month plus (ii) eighty percent (80%) of Care Management Revenues during such month in excess of $500,000. HPS shall pay such amount to SHPS within fifteen days following the end of the applicable month. At the time of payment HPS shall submit to SHPS a schedule for the month of payment setting forth the calculation of fees payable under this Section 2.1 and Care Management Revenues by Client.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'all' with 'majority' and 'equal' with 'approximately', introducing ambiguity regarding the revenue sharing and commission calculation for new clients. This contradicts the explicit payment terms detailed in the 'Contradicted Text,' which requires precise calculations ('an amount equal to (i) eighty-two and one-half percent (82.5%)'). The original agreement requires precision in payment calculations, while the amendment introduces inexact specifications.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding on the parties and their respective successors and assigns, but neither party may, or shall have the power to, assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall generally be binding on the parties and their respective successors and assigns, but neither party may, or shall have the power to, assign this Agreement without notifying the other party, and consent may be withheld based on reasonable business considerations.
perturbation.explanation: The added word 'generally' weakens the binding nature of the agreement. Replacing 'prior written consent...not be unreasonably withheld' with 'notifying the other party, and consent may be withheld based on reasonable business considerations' changes the process and criteria for assignment, allowing for more reasons to refuse an assignment. This creates an in-text contradiction because the clause now introduces an uncertain assignment process where previously it was explicitly stated that consent could not be unreasonably withheld. Specifically this perturbation contradicts Section 4.7 'Where agreement, approval, acceptance, consent, or similar action by either party is required by any provision of this Agreement, such action shall not be unreasonably delayed or withheld.' as the new text implies there may be additional 'reasonable business considerations' to consider
perturbation.contradicted_text: Where agreement, approval, acceptance, consent, or similar action by either party is required by any provision of this Agreement, such action shall not be unreasonably delayed or withheld.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that consent for assignment shall not be unreasonably withheld. The changed text introduces the possibility that consent may be withheld based on 'reasonable business considerations,' which broadens the scope of permissible reasons for withholding consent. This directly contradicts the contradicted text: 'Where agreement, approval, acceptance, consent, or similar action by either party is required by any provision of this Agreement, such action shall not be unreasonably delayed or withheld.' The change removes the explicit limitation on withholding consent, creating a strong in-text contradiction. As per the perturbation explanation, the added word 'generally' weakens the binding nature of the agreement. Replacing 'prior written consent...not be unreasonably withheld' with 'notifying the other party, and consent may be withheld based on reasonable business considerations' changes the process and criteria for assignment, allowing for more reasons to refuse an assignment, creating an ambiguity - in text contradiction.
